_meta.title: The Spell Brigade — Mechanics Specification
_meta.purpose: Foundational reference describing the stat aggregation system, damage pipeline, element effectiveness, modifier hierarchy, and combat resolution. All other gameplay docs (damage-calculation guide, spell, artifact, infusion docs) depend on the definitions here.
_meta.extraction_approach: Class signatures and field offsets were extracted directly from the IL2CPP dump. Method bodies are not present in the dump (only signatures + RVA), so any documented arithmetic is inferred from method names, the single embedded constant `StatCalculator.Exponent = 0.5714286` (~= 4/7), the modifier class hierarchy, and the calls visible in IPlayerStats / HealthContainer / Hitbox. Each inferred formula is tagged with a confidence level (high | inferred | guess).
verification_notes: Re-grepped `enum StatType` → unique hit at line 314327.
Re-grepped `enum ElementType` → unique hit at line 272226; 21 entries including None (so 20 "real" elements).
Re-grepped `class StatCalculator` → static class at line 314227 with three private aggregation methods + the embedded Exponent constant.

stat_types.name: Damage
stat_types.description: Per-spell damage multiplier. Aggregated as a `1 + Σ%` factor against the spell base damage.

stat_types.name: Range
stat_types.description: Projectile / area range. Logarithmic falloff prevents stacking from running away.

stat_types.name: FireRate
stat_types.description: Spell cadence (shots per unit time, or 1 / interval).

stat_types.name: Speed
stat_types.description: Projectile travel speed.

stat_types.name: LifeTime
stat_types.description: Duration that a spell / projectile / zone persists.

stat_types.name: DamageTickRate
stat_types.description: How often a sustained spell ticks damage on overlapping enemies.

stat_types.name: Size
stat_types.description: Spell scale (radius, beam width, etc.). Logarithmic — strongly diminishing returns.

stat_types.name: MovementSpeed
stat_types.description: Player walking speed multiplier.

stat_types.name: MaxHealth
stat_types.description: Player max-HP multiplier (applied to character base MaxHealth).

stat_types.name: Luck
stat_types.description: Flat luck stat. Influences rarity rolls (drops, store items).

stat_types.name: XPGain
stat_types.description: XP gained from enemy kills.

stat_types.name: PickupRadius
stat_types.description: Radius at which XP orbs and pickups are attracted.

stat_types.name: CriticalChance
stat_types.description: Percent chance to crit (0–100). Compared against a random roll per hit (see PlayerSpellDamageInfo).

stat_types.name: CriticalDamage
stat_types.description: Crit damage bonus (additive percent). On a crit, damage is multiplied by (1 + CriticalDamage/100).

stat_types.name: Armor
stat_types.description: Flat damage reduction. Used by PlayerHealthContainer.CalculateDamageAfterArmor.

stat_types.name: Dodge
stat_types.description: Percent chance to ignore an incoming hit (PlayerHealthContainer.HasDodged).

stat_types.name: HealthRegeneration
stat_types.description: HP regenerated every 3s (TimeBetweenHealthRegenerations const on PlayerHealthContainer).

stat_types.name: Rerolls
stat_types.description: Number of store-reroll uses available per shop.

stat_types.name: ReviveSpeed
stat_types.description: Multiplier on how fast a downed teammate is revived.

stat_types.name: Revives
stat_types.description: Number of self-revives remaining.

stat_types.name: Projectiles
stat_types.description: Flat additional projectiles per cast.

stat_types.name: HealOnDodge
stat_types.description: HP healed each time the player dodges (PlayerHealthContainer.CheckHealOnDodgeArtifactEffect).

stat_types.name: HealOnLevelUp
stat_types.description: HP healed each time the player levels up.

stat_types.name: DropChance
stat_types.description: Multiplier on enemy drop probability.

stat_types.name: TerrainSpeedMultiplier
stat_types.description: Movement-speed multiplier while on terrain modifiers (e.g., mud, water).

stat_types.name: HitboxSizeMultiplier
stat_types.description: Scales the player hitbox; usually pushed by negative curses or specific artifacts.

stat_types.name: ElementWeaknessMultiplier
stat_types.description: Bonus damage taken from elemental hits (applied target-side). Used by ElementEffectivenessCalculator.GetElementWeaknessMultiplier.

stat_types.name: ElementStrengthMultiplier
stat_types.description: Bonus damage dealt with elemental (non-None) spells (applied attacker-side). Used by ElementEffectivenessCalculator.GetElementStrengthMultiplier.

stat_types.name: HealingMultiplier
stat_types.description: Multiplier on all outgoing/incoming healing (PlayerSpellDamageInfo carries this for heal-on-damage variants).

stat_types.name: XPGainOnDamageTaken
stat_types.description: XP awarded as a function of damage absorbed (some curse-balancing artifacts).

stat_types.name: HealthPotionAmountMultiplier
stat_types.description: Multiplier on HP restored by collected potions.

stat_types.name: ReviveZoneSize
stat_types.description: Radius of the zone in which a player can revive a teammate.

stat_types.name: GoldGain
stat_types.description: Multiplier on gold gained from any source.

stat_types.name: HealthPercentageOnRevive
stat_types.description: Percent of MaxHealth restored on revive (used both by Self-Revive and Revive-by-teammate).

stat_types.name: Bans
stat_types.description: Bans available per shop (permanently remove an item from this run's store rolls).

stat_types.name: Pins
stat_types.description: Pins available per shop (keep an item for the next visit).

stat_type_gaps.interpretation: Likely deprecated/cut stats; the enum reserves the numeric slots so save data stays stable. Treat as unused.

stat_display_types.name: ModifierPercent
stat_display_types.description: Shown as a `+X%` modifier (e.g., +25% Damage). Used for almost all PercentAdd/Logarithmic stats.

stat_display_types.name: Value
stat_display_types.description: Shown as a plain integer/float (e.g., Armor: 4). Used for Flat stats whose unit is a count.

stat_display_types.name: ValuePercent
stat_display_types.description: Shown as `X%` where the number IS a percentage (CriticalChance 35%).

stat_calculation_types.name: PercentAdd
stat_calculation_types.explanation: All matching modifiers are summed as additive percentages, then applied multiplicatively to base. A Damage modifier of `0.25` and one of `0.5` yield `base * (1 + 0.75) = 1.75x`.
stat_calculation_types.evidence: Method name + lambda `<CalculatePercentAddValue>b__2_0(StatModifier modifier) => modifier.GetValue()` shows a plain sum of GetValue() across the list, then arithmetic with baseValue. This is the canonical Vampire-Survivors-style `1 + sum%` aggregation.

stat_calculation_types.name: Flat
stat_calculation_types.explanation: All matching modifiers are summed, then added to the base value. Used for absolute-count stats (CriticalChance%, Armor points, Rerolls, Projectiles, HealOnDodge…).
stat_calculation_types.evidence: Method name + lambda `<CalculateFlatValue>b__3_0(StatModifier modifier) => modifier.GetValue()`. Distinct method from PercentAdd, and the modifier list reduction is summing.

stat_calculation_types.name: PercentAddLogarithmic
stat_calculation_types.explanation: Same additive sum as PercentAdd but the resulting (1+Σ) factor is raised to an Exponent of `0.5714286` (≈ 4/7) BEFORE being multiplied by base. So 100% of nominal Size modifiers yields `base * 2^0.5714 ≈ 1.487x`, 300% yields `base * 4^0.5714 ≈ 2.21x`. This is the classic concave-falloff curve.
stat_calculation_types.alternative_formula_candidate: value = baseValue * (1 + (Σ modifier.GetValue()) ^ 0.5714286). Less likely because raising the entire `1+Σ` factor matches Risk-of-Rain-style stacking which the existing const value `0.5714286` cleanly mirrors.
stat_calculation_types.evidence: The class contains exactly one float constant — `Exponent = 0.5714286` — and only one method that differs from CalculatePercentAddValue is `CalculatePercentAddLogarithmicValue`. The decompiler-visible lambda (`<CalculatePercentAddLogarithmicValue>b__4_0`) sums modifier values identically to PercentAdd, so the difference must be the post-sum exponentiation against the documented constant.
stat_calculation_types.implication_for_balance: Stats wired to PercentAddLogarithmic (Range, Size) deliberately diminish: a player stacking 600% Size still only gets ~7x scale, not 7x. Designers chose this for stats whose runaway stacking would break the moment-to-moment game loop.

element_types.name: None
element_types.description: Raw/non-elemental. ElementEffectivenessCalculator skips strength/weakness multipliers for None.

element_types.name: Fire
element_types.description: Single-element Fire infusion. Applies FireEffect → burning DOT (FireEffectConfiguration: Damage, NumberOfBurns, SecondsBetweenBurns).

element_types.name: FireDouble
element_types.description: Double Fire infusion (Fire infused into a Fire spell).

element_types.name: Lightning
element_types.description: Single Lightning infusion. LightningEffect chains via NumberOfLightningRicochets dealing LightningDamageFactor of base damage.

element_types.name: Acid
element_types.description: Single Acid infusion. AcidEffect applies ResistanceReduction (target takes more damage) for AcidDuration seconds.

element_types.name: Ice
element_types.description: Single Ice infusion. IceEffect applies a chill/slow scaled by ChillDamageFactor over ChillDuration.

element_types.name: Dark
element_types.description: Single Dark infusion. DarkEffect applies corruption per DarkEffectConfiguration (CorruptionDamageFactor + CorruptionElementBehaviour).

element_encoding_notes.double_meaning: An '<Element>Double' value means the spell was infused with the same element a second time (e.g., a Fire-infused Fire spell). This typically intensifies the element's per-hit effect (stronger DOT, more ricochets, longer chill).
element_encoding_notes.dual_combo_meaning: A two-element name (alphabetically Acid < Dark < Fire < Ice < Lightning in the enum, with the lower-id element listed first when forming a combo identifier) means the spell's pure element was infused with a different element. Both element effects can fire.
element_encoding_notes.extension_method: ElementTypeExtensions.IsInfusedWith(ElementType element, ElementType elementToCheck) — line 272261 — answers whether a packed ElementType contains a given component element.

rarity_types.name: Common
rarity_types.description: Default tier. Lowest store-item value via ImprovementValueContainer.GetValue.

fields.name: Element
fields.description: Element of the damage event (None for non-elemental sources like environment, falls).

fields.name: Damage
fields.description: Nominal damage before target-side adjustments. Set when the DamageInfo is created at the cast site.

fields.name: Direction
fields.description: Optional incoming direction (used for hit-feedback, directional pop-ups, knock-backs).

fields.name: EffectiveDamageDone
fields.description: Final damage that actually got removed from the target — populated after armor/dodge/crit/element resolution. Read by damage-popup, analytics, and damage-dealt events.

methods.name: ContainsDark
methods.description: Convenience: true if the packed Element contains Dark (Dark/DarkDouble/DarkFire/DarkLightning/AcidDark/DarkIce).

fields.name: OwnerClientId
fields.description: Who dealt the damage (network client id).

fields.name: CriticalChance
fields.description: Snapshot of the attacker's CriticalChance stat at hit time.

fields.name: CriticalDamage
fields.description: Snapshot of the attacker's CriticalDamage stat at hit time.

fields.name: WillHealBecauseOfVariant
fields.description: Pre-flagged when a spell variant converts damage to healing (e.g., a healing-on-hit infusion).

subclasses.usage: Created whenever a player-owned source produces damage. The crit roll and conversion logic uses the snapshotted fields, not live stats — so mid-flight, cached stats are used.

fields.name: Spell
fields.description: Which spell produced this hit (used for per-spell analytics + Variant routing).

fields.name: HealingMultiplier
fields.description: Outgoing healing scale if the damage gets converted to healing.

fields.name: SpellId
fields.description: Per-cast identity so e.g. Damage-Tick spells don't double-credit per-hit triggers.

methods_of_note: ConvertDamageToHealing() — returns float of healing produced from this damage event
subclasses.usage: The default damage path for player spells (orb, boomerang, beam, summon, etc.).
Used when a spell explodes; constructed from a source PlayerSpellDamageInfo to preserve crit/spell/owner data.
Damage from player-triggered explosions that aren't spell instances (e.g., Ascension explode-on-revive, ExplodeOnDeath trait).
Damage dealt at respawn (Acolyte ghost explosion / I-frames trigger).

fields.name: WasLethal
fields.description: True if the return-damage kill the attacker

subclasses.usage: Reflected damage when a player has Return-Damage-When-Hit trait.
Damage dealt by a corrupted enemy that's been turned to attack other enemies (Dark element corruption). Attributed back to the corrupting player.
Damage dealt by an enemy to a player.
Damage dealt by a fired enemy projectile (used to distinguish from contact damage in dodge / armor logic).
Damage from world hazards (lava, fall, etc.). Honors HealthContainer.IgnoreLavaDamage and similar.
Generic explosion damage, with separate scalars for enemies and players (so the same explosion can be friendly-fired with FriendlyFireMultiplier = 0.25).
Special damage type generated when a curse rerolls / reduces player HP.
Self-damage event triggered by the `TakeDamageOnLevelUp` curse on each level-up.

methods.name: GetValue
methods.description: Current numeric contribution. Dynamic modifiers can return different values across frames.

methods.name: GetStatType
methods.description: Which Stat this modifier feeds into.

methods.name: Dispose
methods.description: Override to unhook event listeners (DynamicStatModifier subclasses subscribe to game events).

subclasses.description: Marks modifiers applied at character/spell equip time. Stat.GetSumOfNonStartingModifiers() filters these out, so in-game UI can show 'what you gained this run' separately from baseline kit.
Same numeric semantics as StaticStatModifier but not shown in any UI breakdown. Used by internal balancing modifiers the player shouldn't see.

static_branch.usage: Created by data-driven content: SpellConfiguration.StartingModifiers, ImprovementStoreItemData (improvement store purchases), Infusion data, curse stack data. Once added, the Value never recomputes.

fields.name: Parameters
fields.description: Constructor-supplied coefficients. Subclass decides how to interpret each index (typically [0] = scalar per stack, [1] = cap, etc.).

methods.name: GetModifierType
methods.description: Character (applied to PlayerStats) or Spell (applied to a single equipped SpellAttributes block).

artifact_subclasses.trigger: Per-frame: returns current sum of party member levels × Parameters[0].
Conditional: returns Parameters[0] when PlayerHealthContainer.CurrentHealth < Parameters[1] threshold else 0.
Counts side objectives completed; Dispose unsubscribes IAmObjective callback.
Counts player revives performed; subscribes OnPlayerRevived.
Reads PlayerMotor for movement state each frame.
Reads current Armor stat each evaluation.
Reads current Armor; usually negative trade-off.
Counts infusions performed by the player; OnImprovementAdded hook.
Counts shops not rerolled; subscribes OnStoreNotRerolled.
Reads current HP ratio each evaluation (inverse relationship).

spell_variant_subclasses.trigger: True while player stands in a Bell spell's area (IPlayersInBellZoneManager).
Reads PlayerHealthContainer current HP ratio; inverse relation.
Reads PlayerMotor; multiplier active while motionless.
Like above but uses an explicit damageIncrease field; ramps over the standing-still duration.
Effective only while ProximitySpell counts enemies above its threshold.

dynamic_branch.addition_notes: Some `Spell`-typed modifiers above declare GetStatType slot 7 pointing at the same RVA as the simple Damage getter (0x679900) — meaning multiple modifiers reuse a tiny accessor function. This is normal IL2CPP function-deduplication, not a bug.

public_method.name: CalculateEffectiveness
public_method.signature: float CalculateEffectiveness(float value, DamageInfo damageInfo, HealthContainer targetHealthContainer)
public_method.formula_inferred: value' = value * GetElementStrengthMultiplier(damageInfo.OwnerClientId, damageInfo.Element) * GetElementWeaknessMultiplier(target's player owner, damageInfo.Element)
logic_path: 1) If damageInfo.Element == None → return value unchanged (no elemental scaling).
2) ApplyAttackerElementStrength: looks up the attacker's ElementStrengthMultiplier stat (StatType 30) via the player manager and multiplies.

3) ApplyTargetElementWeakness: if target is a player (i.e., has a player-owning HealthContainer) looks up its ElementWeaknessMultiplier (StatType 27) and multiplies. Enemies generally don't carry these stats.

per_element_effect_classes.effect_summary: Applies a Burn DoT: NumberOfBurns ticks of Damage every SecondsBetweenBurns seconds. Each burn tick can roll a critical if CanCriticalHit.
Applies chill/slow via IceEffectHandler. Chill factor is clamped between MinChillFactor=0.2 and MaxChillFactor=0.72 (i.e. up to 72% slow). At hard freeze the target is fully stopped for the duration.
Chains ricochet damage to nearby enemies, each ricochet doing LightningDamageFactor × original damage. Configured by LightningEffectConfiguration.
Applies a debuff that reduces target damage resistance for AcidDuration seconds (effectively a damage-taken increase amplifier). Also wires a 'weakening' flag for in-area sustain modifiers.

Adds corruption points scaling with CorruptionDamageFactor × damage. When corruption fills, the enemy becomes hostile to other enemies (CorruptionState.AttackOtherEnemies); see HealthContainer.InstantlyCorruptRpc + KillAfterCorruptionExpired.

interface.name: IElementEffect
interface.method: void Execute(IElementEffectHandlers handlers, float damage, DamageInfo damageInfo, HealthContainer target)

critical_hit_system.summary: Critical hits are rolled at hit resolution against the attacker's snapshotted CriticalChance, then the base damage is multiplied by (1 + CriticalDamage/100). Snapshot taken when the PlayerDamageInfo is built — not when the projectile lands.
step_by_step: 1) Spell builds a PlayerSpellDamageInfo passing IPlayerStats — snapshots CriticalChance + CriticalDamage from PlayerStats into the DamageInfo.
2) Hitbox.IsCriticalDamage(playerDamageInfo) rolls GetRandomFloat(0,100) and compares < playerDamageInfo.CriticalChance.
3) If yes, Hitbox.CalculateCriticalDamage(damage, source) returns damage × (1 + CriticalDamage/100).

4) HealthContainer.GetDamageOverrideForDamage receives isCriticalHit and can override the displayed amount via DamageOverrideData (also used to fake the Critical-Chance-Mastery ascension that converts excess crit-chance into damage).
critical_hit_system.weakspot_bonus: Hitboxes typed as HitboxType.Weakspot apply a hard-coded 1.5× multiplier (Hitbox.WeakspotDamageMultiplier const at line 294027). This stacks multiplicatively with crit.

armor_system.summary: Armor is a flat post-everything-else damage reduction applied by PlayerHealthContainer.CalculateDamageAfterArmor before HP removal.
armor_system.alternative_formula_candidate: damage_after_armor = incoming_damage × baseValue / (baseValue + Armor). The flat-subtraction model is more likely because: (a) Armor is a Flat-calc stat (id 14); (b) the artifact `IncreaseDamagePerArmor` exists to convert armor stacks into damage — that trade-off only makes sense if armor's diminishing returns aren't already baked in; (c) similar Vampire-Survivors-derived games use flat subtraction.
notes: Armor is applied AFTER element effectiveness and corruption math (those happen inside HealthContainer.RemoveHealth flow, ahead of PerformHealthRemoval).
Friendly-fire damage is pre-multiplied by const FriendlyFireMultiplier = 0.25 (line 310960) on PlayerHealthContainer.
Damage taken while corrupted is further multiplied by damageTakenWhileCorruptedMultiplier (serialized field on PlayerHealthContainer).
EnemyHealthContainer.CalculateDamageAfterDefensiveUpgrades exists at line 281718 (RVA 0x202D120) but no enemy 'armor' Stat is exposed — likely a hook for boss-specific resistance scripts.

dodge_system.summary: Each incoming hit on a player rolls against Dodge%; if it passes, the hit is completely ignored (zero HP loss) and the PlayerDodgePopup is shown.
dodge_system.formula_inferred: if GetRandomFloat(0,100) < Dodge: ignore damage, fire DodgeAttackEventArgs, heal HealOnDodge HP (if > 0).
notes: Dodge is a Flat-calc stat (StatType 15) capped only by content design (no hard ceiling visible in calculator).

spell_stat_lifecycle.summary: Each spell has a per-cast Stat block built by combining a SpellConfiguration's BaseStats, the spell's StartingModifiers, any character-/spell-improvements picked up, any infusion/variant DynamicStatModifiers, and any artifact DynamicStatModifiers that target Spell scope. The Stat object aggregates by StatCalculationType.
ordered_pipeline: 1) Equip: SpellConfiguration.Stats (List<BaseStat>) → each BaseStat.CreateCalculator() returns a Stat seeded with `baseValue` and the StatType's default StatCalculationType (via StatTypeExtensions.GetCalculationType).
2) SpellConfiguration.StartingModifiers (List<StartingStatModifier>) → IPlayerStats.AddSpellModifier for each — they sit in Stat.Modifiers tagged so GetSumOfNonStartingModifiers can skip them.

3) Improvements picked up in store: ImprovementStoreItemData has a StatType + ImprovementValueContainer that gives a per-rarity float; the chosen value becomes a new StaticStatModifier added via IPlayerStats.AddCharacterImprovement / AddSpellImprovement (StatModifiersType.Static).
4) SpellVariants: each variant unlocks SpellVariantStatModifier_* (dynamic) and/or static stat tweaks; these run AddSpellModifier when the variant is taken.

5) Infusions: AddInfusion(SpellType, ElementType) — does NOT add stat numbers per se; it changes the ElementType on the spell's outgoing DamageInfo. Doubling the same element ('FireDouble') is what intensifies the IElementEffect.
6) Artifacts: equipping an artifact registers its ArtifactStatModifier_* instances. Character-scope ones go on PlayerStats; Spell-scope ones go on the relevant SpellAttributes.

7) Curses: each Curse stack pushes (typically negative) StaticStatModifiers on character-level stats (e.g., StrongerEnemies bumps an enemy global), or installs hooks that take damage (TakeDamageOnLevelUp).
8) At evaluation time, Stat.GetValue() calls StatCalculator.Calculate(baseValue, Modifiers, statCalculationType) — see stat_calculation_types for the per-type math.
spell_stat_lifecycle.value_with_extra_modifiers: Stat.GetValueWithExtraModifiers(extras) lets callers do a what-if computation (e.g., store preview: 'what would Damage be if I bought this item?') without mutating the Stat.

damage_formula_end_to_end.summary: Synthesized chain from spell-cast to HP-removal, combining StatCalculator, ElementEffectivenessCalculator, Hitbox crit logic, and PlayerHealthContainer reductions.
pseudo_code: # 1. Spell evaluates its current Damage stat at cast time
= spellBaseDamage * (1 + sum(% Damage modifiers from improvements + artifacts + variant + character-damage))
# 2. Construct the DamageInfo, snapshotting crit fields
# 3. Hit lands. Spell-specific multipliers (e.g., DealsMoreDamageTowardsCenter, weakspot)
raw = raw * (1 + info.CriticalDamage / 100) if isCrit else raw
# 6. Per-element damage multipliers from per-target debuffs (acid resistance reduction, dark corruption mod)
# 7. Target-specific defensive math (armor / dodge for players, contextual scaling for enemies)
if HasDodged(): return 0   # popup + dodge event
if attacker is teammate: raw *= 0.25  # FriendlyFireMultiplier

step_confidences.what: Crit values snapshot into DamageInfo at creation
step_confidences.evidence: PlayerDamageInfo fields CriticalChance/CriticalDamage exist and are set by .ctor(ulong ownerClientId, SpellType, IPlayerStats).
Hitbox.CalculateCriticalDamage exists with PlayerDamageInfo source; the only crit field on source is CriticalDamage; ValuePercent display type implies %.
ElementEffectivenessCalculator has those two named helpers and they read StatType 27/30.
HealthContainer.GetDamageAfterMultiplier + CalculateDamageAfterResistances are both concrete methods invoked in the removal pipeline.
See armor_system, dodge_system rationale; matches Vampire-Survivors-style genre.

open_questions: Order of operations between target's DamageResistance vs Armor vs corruption multiplier — only the existence of each method is dump-visible, not the call ordering inside RemoveHealth.
Whether the `damageMultiplierProviders` list (per-HealthContainer IDamageMultiplierProvider references) is summed multiplicatively or just product-reduced.

Whether the crit roll uses a uniform 0..100 or a 0..1 scale; the helper Hitbox.GetRandomFloat(min,max) is generic. Comparing against `CriticalChance` (which is stored as the same number players see in UI) strongly implies 0..100.
Whether StatCalculator preserves NaN / negative bases (e.g., do negative cursed Damage stats clamp to zero before multiplication?). No clamping is visible in the dump.

curse_system.summary: Curses are stackable difficulty modifiers applied at run start. Each Curse has a CurseIdentifier and NumberOfStacks. A CurseConfiguration holds a list of CurseStackConfigurations; each stack pushes its own StatModifier(s) onto the player/world.

curse_identifiers.name: EnemiesDropLessXP
curse_identifiers.description: Multiplier on XP from enemy kills (negative XPGain or world-side bias).

curse_identifiers.name: OneHitMode
curse_identifiers.description: Any damage taken depletes HP (PlayerHealthContainer.HasOneHitModeCurse → forces lethal).

curse_identifiers.name: OneLessStoreItem
curse_identifiers.description: Stocks one fewer item per shop.

curse_identifiers.name: TakeDamageOnLevelUp
curse_identifiers.description: Each level-up deals self-damage via TakeDamageOnLevelUpCurseDamageInfo.

curse_system.stacking_model: Curse is a struct-like data {Id, NumberOfStacks}. CurseConfiguration.GetCurseLevelForStack(int stackNumber) picks the correct CurseStackConfiguration; that stack's payload (added through ICanManageImprovements.AddCurses → typically AddStatModifiers) layers on top of whatever's already there.

rarity_value_system.summary: Improvement store items carry an ImprovementValueContainer with four serialized floats (common / rare / epic / legendary). The chosen rarity selects which float becomes the Value of the StaticStatModifier added to the player.
rarity_value_system.method: ImprovementValueContainer.GetValue(RarityType) at line 342913 returns the chosen tier's float.
rarity_value_system.luck_influence_hypothesis: RandomRarityPicker implements ICanPickARarity (line 340002); the player's Luck stat (Flat, id 9) is the most likely input that biases its rarity-distribution roll, though the dump doesn't expose the exact weighting curve.

upgrade_tier_system.summary: Upgrade tiers represent the player's permanent meta-progression (Spells / Artifacts / Characters all unlock and reach higher tiers across runs). The in-run improvement system above is orthogonal — it only stacks within a single run.
notes: Per-content unlock gating is via challenges (SpellToChallengeMap on SpellConfiguration; ChallengeId on CurseConfiguration.GetUnlockedStacks).
ImprovementStoreItemData / its subclasses host the per-rarity values (see rarity_value_system); the same item type can drop at different rarities, the value is interpolated at pick time.

DynamicStatModifierType.name: Character
DynamicStatModifierType.description: Modifier feeds the player-level Stat list (e.g., character-wide Damage).

DynamicStatModifierType.name: Spell
DynamicStatModifierType.description: Modifier feeds a particular equipped spell's Stat list.

StatModifiersType.name: Static
StatModifiersType.description: Standard improvement: StaticStatModifier created at pick time.

StatModifiersType.name: Starting
StatModifiersType.description: Tagged as character/spell baseline so it can be filtered out of post-run displays.

CorruptionElementBehaviour.name: UseCoreElement
CorruptionElementBehaviour.description: Corrupted enemy attacks use the originating spell's core element.

CorruptionElementBehaviour.name: UseNoElement
CorruptionElementBehaviour.description: Corrupted enemy attacks deal non-elemental damage.

CorruptionElementBehaviour.name: UseDarkElement
CorruptionElementBehaviour.description: Corrupted enemy attacks always deal Dark damage.
